projectDescription: HackGATE is a managed security proxy gateway that lets organizations stand up controlled, authenticated test environments for penetration testing and bug bounty programs — without exposing production systems directly.
keyFeatures: One-click HackGATE site creation — proxies your origin through a managed, hackgate.io subdomain
Hacker allowlist — restrict site access to approved researcher emails or open to all
Rate limiting — configurable per-site request rate policies
Block rules — path-based HTTP method blocking with per-rule enable/disable toggle
Traffic analytics — most active users, path discovery, traffic timeline via Elasticsearch
WAF monitoring — ModSecurity-powered attack type breakdown and rules timeline
API coverage — upload an OpenAPI schema and see which endpoints hackers have exercised
Scheduled start/stop — set future activation and deactivation times for a site
PIE security checks — built-in checks for common vulnerabilities (SQLi, path traversal, .git exposure, Log4Shell, etc.)
Project management — group HackGATE sites into named projects with timeline and metadata
Organization API key support — authenticate with Clerk org API keys for CI/CD and automation
Billing information management — store and update company billing details per organization
publicApiSurface: GET /api/sites — list all HackGATE sites for the authenticated organization
POST /api/sites — create a new HackGATE site (proxied subdomain)
GET /api/sites/{id} — get a specific site
GET /api/sites/enable/{id} — enable (activate) a site
GET /api/sites/disable/{id} — disable (deactivate) a site
GET /api/sites/getHackers/{id} — list allowed hackers for a site
POST /api/sites/addHacker — add a hacker to the allowlist
POST /api/sites/removeHacker — remove a hacker from the allowlist
POST /api/sites/allowedHackerType — toggle between custom allowlist and open access
POST /api/sites/setRateLimiting — configure rate limiting policy for a site
POST /api/sites/start — schedule a site to start at a future time
POST /api/sites/stop — schedule a site to stop at a future time
POST /api/sites/instructions — update hacker instructions for a site
POST /api/sites/credentials — update test credentials for a site
GET /api/sites/{id}/blocklist — retrieve block rules for a site
POST /api/sites/blocklist — update block rules for a site
POST /api/sites/uploadOpenAPISchema — upload an OpenAPI JSON/YAML schema for a site
GET /api/sites/getAPIdefinition/{id} — retrieve the processed OpenAPI definition
GET /api/sites/getLog/{id} — get activity log for a site
GET /api/sites/getUsageByMonth/{id} — get usage day count for a month
GET /api/projects — list projects
POST /api/projects — create a project
PUT /api/projects — update a project
GET /api/projects/getProjectItems — list project items
POST /api/projects/createProjectItem — create a project item
PUT /api/projects/updateProjectItem — update a project item
GET /api/analytics/getMostActiveUsers — top users by request count
GET /api/analytics/getTotalRequest — total request count for a site and time range
GET /api/analytics/getPathDiscovery — discovered request paths
GET /api/analytics/getTrafficTimeline — per-user traffic timeline
GET /api/analytics/getWAFMessages — WAF rule trigger messages
GET /api/analytics/getWAFAttackTypes — WAF attack type breakdown
GET /api/analytics/getAPICoverage — API endpoint coverage vs OpenAPI schema
GET /api/organization — get organization credits
GET /api/billing — retrieve billing information
POST /api/billing — update billing information
GET /api/account/currentUser — get or create current user profile
GET /api/PIE/getChecks — list available PIE security checks
POST /api/PIE/runCheck — run a PIE security check against a site
